content:
Hello there Dwilhelm.


Technical Support: Thunderbird Connection Issues This indicates a communication error between thunderbird.exe and the mail server. This is often caused by a hung process within the system or the software itself.

1. Basic Troubleshooting

Have you tried restarting Thunderbird? Completely close the application and reopen it. Additionally, check if Offline Mode is active:

Look for the icon (two small computers) in the bottom-left corner.

If there is a red "X" over the icon, you are working offline.

Click the icon to toggle back to Online Mode.

2. Check for Zombie Processes

Check for any hung processes. When closing Thunderbird, "zombie processes" can sometimes remain active in the background, preventing the application from establishing a new connection. Restart your computer to ensure all instances of the software are fully terminated.

3. Review Account & Security Settings

If the issue persists, please check your SMTP and Server Settings:

Ensure that "Check for new messages at startup" and "Check for new messages every X minutes" are enabled.

Clear your password cache: Providers occasionally update their authentication methods (e.g., switching to OAuth2). Cached credentials can conflict with these new security protocols, causing the connection to fail.

If the steps above failed, please use the global database to rebuild the involved data. https://support.mozilla.org/en-US/kb/rebuilding-global-database
creator: Googlethunderbird.

creator: dwilhelm

content:
Hello there



Since the downloads are visible in the web app, it’s possible the desktop software is glitching or the view settings have changed. We can start with a 'soft' fix by repairing the folder. Right-click the Inbox folder, select Properties, and on the General Information tab, click Repair Folder. Don’t worry, your emails will remain safe. Fix this Alternatively, check your view settings. Go to the View menu and select Messages. Ensure All is selected. If 'Threads with Unread' is active, most of your mail may be hidden. , <Ensure that All is selected. If "Threads with Unread" or "Watched Threads with Unread" is selected, most of your mail will disappear.> .

Use Change the protocol from POP to IMAP. The issues you're seeing in Thunderbird suggest that using POP on the desktop may have triggered a server-side conflict

Tip Verify your Thunderbird download location: Settings > General > Files & Attachments > Save files to..

content:
Per poter inquadrare il problema prova a fornire queste informazioni:

  1. Versione di Thunderbird
  2. Versione del sistema operativo
  3. Provider delle caselle email (Gmail, Libero, Hotmail, etc.)
  4. Tipologia di account (POP3, IMAP)
  5. Estensioni installate
  6. Messaggi di errore che vengono visualizzati durante l'utilizzo di Thunderbird
  7. Software di sicurezza eventualmente presenti (Antivirus e simili)
  8. Screenshot del problema (se utile a chiarire il contesto)

content:
By default Thunderbird creates two standard folders in the "Local Folders": Trash (in the English-US) version or Deleted (in the English-GB version) Outbox which is the temporary folder for messages to be sent later (first image)

So normally you won't see a Sent folder in Local folders, unless ..... • you have POP accounts for which you chose the "Global Inbox (Local Folders)" in Account settings > Server-Settings > Advanced ... or • you chose to place a copy in ◉ "Sent" folder in Local Folders in Account settings > account name > Copies & Folders (second image)

Unfortunately your description lacks clarity, so I have difficulties to imagine your Thunderbird installation. One or more accounts? POP or IMAP? Maybe a screen shot of your folder pane would help.

content: My first suggestion is to see if your email provider offers a filtering ability or a blacklist. That is preferred, as that would prevent the message from ever reaching Thunderbird. Failing that option, I suggest creating a filter at tools>message filters to delete the message upon receipt.

That has caused confusion and frustration. I would suggest rightclicking the account in folder pane, select 'subscribe' and untick the folders not used.

content:
Are you talking about a POP mail account? 'Account Settings' > 'Disk Space' for account "To save disk space, do not download:" Below that text is the checkbox 'Messages larger than X kb' where you set the size as well. I presume you selected that checkbox and set a size. What size did you set - it helps when you need to look for the info below?

Settings > General scroll down to bottom click on 'Config Editor' button Advanced Preferences opens in a new tab In search type: .max_size There should a default which you ignore

  • mail.server.default.max_size

look for lines like this where line same but with a different server number: examples:

  • mail.server.server1.max_size
  • mail.server.server2.max_size

They should have a value of whatever you set. For any of those lines with server number - click on the far right delete/trash bin icon. When you have trashed them - they now have a strikethrough line. Exit Thunderbird wait a few moments and then restart Thunderbird. It will completely remove them and update preferences properly and should reolve the problem.

I did choose the option like you and then later unchecked it. I expected those Advanced Preferences to be removed auto matically and was surprised to they still existed - so I removed everything via the above method. This maybe something to get reported as a bug, so I'll check to see if it's been previously reported.
